rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Wacker-type aerobic oxygenation of triaryl-substituted alkenes to 1,2,2-triarylethanones using O2 as oxygen source in an undivided cell with LiClO4/CH3CN
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the triaryl alkene to a radical cation (Eox ~2.1 V vs SCE); simultaneous cathodic reduction of O2 to superoxide (Ered ~ -0.91 V vs SCE)

key_intermediates.name: zwitterionic peroxide adduct from radical cation + superoxide (4)

electron_flow: At the anode, the triaryl alkene undergoes single-electron oxidation to a radical cation; at the cathode, O2 is reduced to superoxide. Superoxide couples with the radical cation to give a zwitterion that cyclizes to a 1,2-dioxetane, which fragments with a 1,2-aryl shift to deliver the 1,2,2-triarylethanone (and an epoxide that further rearranges to the same ketone).

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product 2a formed, indicating involvement of a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No 2a formed without applied current, confirming electrochemical activation is required

probe_reagents.name: ultra-dry conditions
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 2a unchanged (71%), excluding H2O as oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: 18O2
probe_reagents.observation: HRMS shows 18O-labeled 2a, confirming O2 as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O under Ar
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed, excluding water as oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: O2 reduced at -0.91 V vs SCE; 1a oxidized at ~2.1 V vs SCE, supporting paired anodic/cathodic events

notes: Authors invoke a 1,2-dioxetane intermediate that fragments with 1,2-aryl migration of the electron-rich aryl group to rationalize the 1,2,2-triarylethanone connectivity; an alternative pathway via an epoxide intermediate (6) that rearranges (Meinwald-type) is proposed as a parallel route to the same ketone. No DFT or KIE reported.
